commands.review_summary:
The PR guards export HTML template content filtering and iteration against non-array values and adds malformed-content regression tests.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +49. Total +52 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main calls `.filter()` or iterates directly on `msg.content` and `result.content`, so null or scalar persisted transcript content can throw during export rendering. I did not run tests because this review is read-only and tests can create artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer proof follow-up is needed; there is no concrete code repair for ClawSweeper to make from the current review.

target.title: fix(export-html): guard msg.content and result.content filter/iteration paths against non-array values
target.body:
Closes #88255

## Summary

Five call sites in `src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.js` called `.filter()` or iterated with `for...of` directly on `msg.content` or `result.content` without first checking `Array.isArray`. When a persisted transcript row carries a non-array content value (`null`, `undefined`, or any scalar), those paths throw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'filter')`.

Fixed sites (all in the same source template file):

1. `computeStats()` — `msg.content.filter(...)` stats path (assistant messages)
2. `renderEntry()` assistant branch — two `for (const block of msg.content)` render loops
3. `renderEntry()` user branch — `content.filter(...)` text extraction fallback for non-string content
4. `renderToolCall()` — `result.content.filter(...)` text and image accessors

All fixed with `Array.isArray(x) ? x : []` normalization. Regression tests added for assistant and user message cases.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `computeStats()` and `renderEntry()` in the export HTML template crash with `TypeError: msg.content.filter is not a function` when a transcript message row has non-array content (null, undefined, or scalar). The fix guards all four unguarded call sites in the source template.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0, commit `c5a78001e51a11b3602d5ab1a81c8b9a6be6d2c0`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1 — reproduce the crash on the unguarded path (pre-fix baseline):
```
node -e "const msg={role:'assistant',content:null}; msg.content.filter(c=>c.type==='toolCall')"
```

Step 2 — run the export template directly (loads `template.js` via Node.js vm, parses rendered HTML with linkedom — same execution path as the real export):
```
node --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import fs from "node:fs"; import vm from "node:vm"; import path from "node:path"; import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
const dir = "src/auto-reply/reply/export-html";
const tmplJs = fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir,"template.js"),"utf8");
const markedJs = fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir,"vendor/marked.min.js"),"utf8");
const hlJs = fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir,"vendor/highlight.min.js"),"utf8");
const tmplHtml = fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir,"template.html"),"utf8");
const {parseHTML} = await import("linkedom");
const ts = "2026-05-30T07:00:00.000Z";
const session = {header:{id:"proof",timestamp:ts},entries:[{id:"1",parentId:null,timestamp:ts,type:"message",message:{role:"user",content:null}},{id:"2",parentId:"1",timestamp:ts,type:"message",message:{role:"assistant",content:null}}],leafId:"2",systemPrompt:"",tools:[]};
const enc = Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(session),"utf8").toString("base64");
const html = [["CSS",""],["SESSION_DATA",enc],["MARKED_JS",""],["HIGHLIGHT_JS",""],["JS",""]].reduce((h,[n,v])=>h.replace(new RegExp('(<(?:script|style)\\b(?=[^>]*\\bdata-openclaw-export-placeholder="'+n+'")[^>]*>)(</(?:script|style)>)'),'$1'+v+'$2'),tmplHtml);
const {document,window:w} = parseHTML(html);
if(w?.HTMLElement){for(const el of document.querySelectorAll("*"))if(el&&!("scrollIntoView"in el))Object.defineProperty(el,"scrollIntoView",{configurable:true,value:()=>{}});}
const rt={document,console,clearTimeout:()=>{},setTimeout:(fn)=>{fn();return 0;},URLSearchParams,TextDecoder,atob:(s)=>Buffer.from(s,"base64").toString("binary"),btoa:(s)=>Buffer.from(s,"binary").toString("base64"),navigator:{clipboard:{writeText:async()=>{}}},history:{replaceState:()=>{}},location:{href:"http://localhost/export.html",search:""}};
rt.window=rt;rt.self=rt;rt.globalThis=rt;
vm.createContext(rt);vm.runInContext(markedJs,rt);vm.runInContext(hlJs,rt);vm.runInContext(tmplJs,rt);
console.log("template.js rendered OK");console.log("messages div:",!!document.getElementById("messages"));
EOF
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before (unguarded crash on `msg.content.filter`):
```
$ node -e "const msg={role:'assistant',content:null}; msg.content.filter(c=>c.type==='toolCall')"
[eval]:1
const msg={role:'assistant',content:null}; msg.content.filter(c=>c.type==='toolCall')
                                                       ^

TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'filter')
    at [eval]:1:56
Node.js v22.22.0
exit code: 1
```

After (template.js executed via Node.js vm with null content for both user and assistant messages):
```
$ node proof-88255.mjs
--- Proof: export template.js handles null/undefined/scalar content ---
Node.js: v22.22.0

content=null: OK — messages div: true, assistant div: true
content=undefined: OK — messages div: true, assistant div: true
content=number (42): OK — messages div: true, assistant div: true

All cases rendered without crash.
exit code: 0
```

The proof script loaded `template.js`, `marked.min.js`, and `highlight.min.js` from the patched source tree, constructed a linkedom DOM, injected session data with null content for both user and assistant message roles, and executed the full export template rendering pipeline via `vm.runInContext` — the same execution path the real OpenClaw export uses.

**Observed result after fix:** `template.js` renders a complete export HTML page without throwing when assistant and user message content is null, undefined, or a non-array scalar. The `messages` div is present in the rendered output and the assistant message div is rendered (empty content, no crash).

**What was not tested:** Generating and opening the exported HTML file in an actual web browser, and end-to-end export triggered from a running OpenClaw session via the export-session command. The vm-based proof above exercises the same `template.js` code paths as the real export without a headless browser.
target.author: alix-007
